title: Calendar view UI: day, week, month, and custom time-frame views
body:
## Issue description

Nova-Circle currently displays events as simple lists within group detail pages. There is no dedicated calendar view that allows users to visualize their events across time. Users need a calendar interface that supports multiple view modes (day, week, month, custom time frame) to effectively manage their personal activities and see events shared by group members.

### Current state
- Events are displayed as a flat list in the group detail page
- No calendar visualization exists
- No date navigation or time-frame selection exists
- The `CalendarEvent` interface in `client/src/api/events.ts` already has `startAt` and `endAt` fields suitable for calendar rendering

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Calendar component

Build a reusable calendar view component that supports:

**View modes:**
- **Day view** — Single day with hourly time slots, events positioned by start/end time
- **Week view** — 7-day grid with events shown in their respective day columns and time slots
- **Month view** — Traditional month grid with event indicators/titles in each day cell
- **Custom time frame** — User-selectable date range (e.g., 3 days, 2 weeks) with appropriate rendering

**Navigation:**
- Previous/next buttons to navigate between time periods (day, week, month)
- "Today" button to jump to the current date
- Date picker for jumping to a specific date
- View mode selector (day/week/month/custom)

**Event rendering:**
- Events display according to their visibility level:
  - `busy` → opaque block showing "Busy" with owner name, no clickable detail
  - `title` → block showing event title and time
  - `details` → block showing event title, time, and a preview; clickable to full detail
- Personal (unshared) events display with full detail to the owner
- Color-coding or visual indicators to distinguish:
  - Personal events vs. shared events
  - Events from different groups
  - Different visibility levels
- Handle overlapping events gracefully (side-by-side rendering or stacking)
- Handle all-day events (events without `endAt` or spanning full days)

### 2. Data fetching

- Fetch events for the visible time range from the personal events API (`GET /api/v1/events?from=...&to=...`)
- Fetch shared events from each selected group (`GET /api/v1/groups/:groupId/events?from=...&to=...`)
- Merge and deduplicate events (same event shared to multiple groups)
- Implement efficient data fetching — only request data for the visible time window
- Cache recently fetched date ranges to avoid redundant requests during navigation

### 3. Calendar page

- New page/route: `/calendar` (or integrate into main app view)
- Calendar is the primary view for the authenticated user
- Persists user's preferred view mode (day/week/month) in local storage
- Responsive design: month view may simplify on mobile, day view should work well on small screens

### 4. Design requirements

- Follow the project's UI philosophy: simple, calm, aesthetic, practical
- Clean layouts, restrained styling, clear typography
- Use centralized semantic design tokens for colors — no hardcoded colors
- Support light and dark mode
- Ensure accessibility:
  - Keyboard navigation between days/events
  - Screen reader support for event information
  - Sufficient contrast ratios in all themes
  - ARIA labels for calendar navigation

### 5. Interaction patterns

- Click on a time slot → create new personal event at that time (pre-fill date/time)
- Click on an event → navigate to event detail (if visibility level allows)
- Drag to select time range → create new event spanning that range (stretch goal)
- Hover on event → show tooltip with event summary

---

## Additional notes

- Depends on: Personal event management, Event sharing to groups, View group shared events
- Consider using a lightweight calendar library if one fits the project's needs, but evaluate carefully — the project avoids unnecessary dependencies
- The calendar component should be reusable (could be embedded in group detail pages later)
- Performance matters: months with many events should render smoothly
- Follow existing client architecture patterns (React, TypeScript, Vite, Vitest)
- Follow existing component patterns (see `client/src/components/` for conventions)
- The calendar view should integrate with the theme system (ThemeSwitcher)

### Related files
- `client/src/api/events.ts` — event API client
- `client/src/pages/` — existing page structure
- `client/src/components/` — existing component patterns
- `client/src/App.tsx` — routing
- `client/src/theme/` — theme tokens and system

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Calendar page accessible at a dedicated route
- [ ] Day view shows events in hourly time slots
- [ ] Week view shows 7-day grid with events positioned correctly
- [ ] Month view shows traditional month grid with event indicators
- [ ] Custom time-frame view allows user-selected date ranges
- [ ] Navigation: previous, next, today, and date picker work for all view modes
- [ ] View mode selector allows switching between day, week, month, custom
- [ ] Events render according to their visibility level (busy/title/details)
- [ ] Personal events display with full detail to the owner
- [ ] Overlapping events render without hiding content
- [ ] Click on time slot pre-fills event creation with selected date/time
- [ ] Click on event navigates to event detail (respecting visibility)
- [ ] Calendar fetches data efficiently for the visible time range only
- [ ] User's preferred view mode persists across sessions
- [ ] Calendar supports light and dark themes
- [ ] Calendar meets accessibility requirements (keyboard nav, screen readers, contrast)
- [ ] Responsive design works on mobile and desktop
- [ ] Component unit tests cover rendering logic and view mode switching
- [ ] Integration tests cover data fetching and event merging
- [ ] CI pipeline passes
